content.content:
# Subagents Documentation

You can find comprehensive subagents documentation at: **https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents.md**

## Key Topics Covered

The subagents guide includes:

- **What subagents are** and their key benefits
- **Quick start** instructions for getting started
- **Configuration** details including file locations, plugin agents, CLI-based setup, and model selection
- **Management** using the `/agents` command or direct file management
- **Effective usage** patterns like automatic delegation and explicit invocation
- **Built-in subagents** including the Plan subagent
- **Example subagents** such as code reviewer, debugger, and data scientist implementations
- **Best practices** and advanced usage like chaining and dynamic selection
- **Performance considerations** for optimal operation

## Related Resources

Subagents are also mentioned in the broader context of:
- Common workflows documentation
- Plugin and skills configuration
- Output styles and custom commands

The documentation emphasizes that subagents can be configured for specialized tasks and automatically delegated based on your needs.

content.content:
# Subagents: Key Characteristics

## Limitations

The documentation doesn't explicitly list limitations, but reveals several constraints:

- **No spawning**: "subagents cannot spawn other subagents," preventing infinite nesting
- **Read-only constraints vary**: The Explore subagent is "strictly read-only," while others can modify files
- **Context isolation**: Each operates in its own separate context window

## User Interaction

The documentation doesn't address whether subagents can ask users questions. It focuses on delegation and autonomous operation rather than interactive dialogue.

## Tool Access

Subagents can be configured with specific tools. Options include:

- **Inherit all**: Omit the `tools` field for full access, including MCP server tools
- **Selective access**: Specify tools as "comma-separated list" for granular control
- **Available tools**: Read, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, and MCP tools

The `/agents` command provides "interactive interface that lists all available tools."

## Communication Model

The documentation doesn't describe inter-agent communication. Instead, it emphasizes that subagents "work independently and return results" to the main conversation.

## Context Management

Key points:

- Subagents maintain "separate context window" from the main thread
- This prevents "pollution of the main conversation"
- Subagents can be resumed with their full "transcript file," maintaining previous context across invocations

Links: [{"title":"Subagents - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents"},{"title":"Claude Code Frameworks & Sub-Agents: The Complete 2025 Developer's Guide","url":"https://www.medianeth.dev/blog/claude-code-frameworks-subagents-2025"},{"title":"Claude Code: Subagent Deep Dive | Code Centre","url":"https://cuong.io/blog/2025/06/24-claude-code-subagent-deep-dive"},{"title":"Challenges in Multi-Agent Systems : Google A2A, Claude Code & Research, Gödel Agent | by Joyce Birkins | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@joycebirkins/challenges-in-multi-agent-systems-google-a2a-claude-code-research-g%C3%B6del-agent-e2c415e14a5e"},{"title":"Specialized AI Workflows with Claude Code Subagents - lgallardo.com","url":"https://lgallardo.com/2025/08/02/claude-code-subagents-specialized-workflows/"},{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"GitHub - VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents: Production-ready Claude subagents collection with 100+ specialized AI agents for full-stack development, DevOps, data science, and business operations.","url":"https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents"},{"title":"Revolutionizing AI Development: How Claude Code's Sub Agents Transform Task Management","url":"https://htdocs.dev/posts/revolutionizing-ai-development-how-claude-codes-sub-agents-transform-task-management/"},{"title":"Claude Code Subagents for Digital Marketing: Complete 2025 Guide","url":"https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/claude-code-subagents-digital-marketing-guide"},{"title":"Claude Code Subagents: The Revolutionary Multi-Agent Development System That Changes Everything - Cursor IDE 博客","url":"https://www.cursor-ide.com/blog/claude-code-subagents"}]

Based on the search results, here's a comprehensive overview of Claude Code subagents' capabilities and limitations in 2025:

## Capabilities

### Core Features
Custom subagents in Claude Code are specialized AI assistants that can be invoked to handle specific types of tasks. They enable more efficient problem-solving by providing task-specific configurations with customized system prompts, tools and a separate context window.(no content)Claude Code's introduction of sub-agents in July 2025 marked a watershed moment—enabling specialized AI assistants to work collaboratively on complex projects while maintaining independent contexts and expertise areas.

### Parallelization
Claude Code can run up to 10 tasks in parallel, processing them in batches. Perfect for independent subtasks across large codebases.(no content)Claude Code supports up to 10 parallel tasks running concurrently. July 2025 implementations successfully scale beyond this limit using intelligent queuing systems that manage 100+ agents through batch processing.

### Context Management
Subagents enable parallelization: you can spin up multiple subagents to work on different tasks simultaneously. They help manage context: subagents use their own isolated context windows, and only send relevant information back to the orchestrator, rather than their full context. This makes them ideal for tasks that require sifting through large amounts of information where most of it won't be useful.

### Tool Permissions
Each subagent can have different tool access levels, allowing you to limit powerful tools to specific subagent types.(no content)Read-only agents (reviewers, auditors): Read, Grep, Glob - analyze without modifying. Research agents (analysts, researchers): Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch - gather information. Code writers (developers, engineers): Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep - create and execute.

### Recent Updates (October 2025)
Claude Code v2.0.28 (released October 27, 2025) introduces three features that fundamentally change how marketing teams use sub-agents: Plan Mode, sub-agent resumption, and dynamic model selection.

## Limitations

### Coordination Issues
It's important to understand remaining limitations: Subagents don't automatically coordinate. Subagents don't communicate with each other or share context between their spaces.(no content)The core difficulty of Multi-Agent systems lies in the information transfer capability, semantic compression, and decision-making synergy between the main agent and sub-agents, as well as among sub-agents. Agents at the current LRM level are not yet adept at task delegation and real-time division of labor coordination.

### Task Delegation Constraints
Claude Code chooses a deliberately simplified design path: letting sub-agents perform information exploration tasks and avoiding having them take on decision-making or writing work. This also allows the main agent to "move" the sub-agent's history out of the main context, thus preserving more token space for the main task logic.(no content)The sub-agent's responsibility is typically to answer a question, not the main task of writing code. Because this type of sub-agent lacks the full context of the main agent, its decisions might deviate from the main intent unless the question is very clear and specific.

### Parallelization Constraints
Claude Code did not pull more tasks from the queue after a task was done. Instead, it waited for all 4 tasks to finish before starting the next 4 tasks. Different variations of the prompt resulted in the same behavior. It doesn't seem possible to make this more efficient with the current implementation of Claude Code's Task Tool.

### Token Usage
While Claude Code subagents deliver exceptional productivity gains, their parallel nature introduces significant token consumption considerations that require careful management. July 2025 testing data shows that active multi-agent sessions consume approximately 3-4x more tokens than traditional single-threaded interactions. Each subagent maintains an independent 200K token context window, and with 10 agents running concurrently, token usage can escalate rapidly without proper controls.

### Reliability Concerns
And if multiple sub-agents are run simultaneously, their answers often contradict each other, leading to reliability issues.

content.text: Let me also fetch the official Claude Code subagents documentation directly:

input.prompt: What are subagent limitations? Can they ask users questions? What tools do they have? How do they communicate with main agent? Context isolation details?

content.content:
# Subagent Capabilities & Limitations

## User Interaction
The documentation doesn't specify whether subagents can directly ask users questions. They appear to work autonomously within their delegated tasks and return results to the main conversation.

## Available Tools
Subagents can access any of Claude Code's internal tools. The docs state: *"Subagents can be granted access to any of Claude Code's internal tools."* You can either:
- Omit the tools field to inherit all tools from the main thread
- Specify individual tools as a comma-separated list for granular control
- Access MCP tools from configured servers when inheriting all tools

## Context Isolation
Each subagent operates independently with its own separate context window. This prevents task-specific information from polluting the main conversation while keeping high-level objectives focused.

## Communication with Main Agent
The documentation doesn't detail a direct bidirectional communication channel. Subagents work independently and *"return results"* to the primary thread. The main agent can invoke subagents explicitly or through automatic delegation based on task matching.

## Key Limitations
- **No nested delegation**: Subagents cannot spawn other subagents
- **Permission-based constraints**: Subagents can be configured with specific tool access levels
- **Model assignment**: Limited to specified model aliases (sonnet, opus, haiku) or inheriting the main conversation's model

The architecture emphasizes specialized task handling over continuous bidirectional dialogue.

The architecture emphasizes specialized task handling over continuous bidirectional dialogue.

content.text:
Based on my research of Claude Code documentation and related resources, here's a comprehensive overview of subagent limitations and capabilities:

## 1. Can Subagents Ask Questions to Users Directly?

**No** - The official documentation does not indicate that subagents can directly ask users questions. They are designed to work **autonomously** within their delegated tasks and return results to the main conversation. Subagents operate as specialized workers that receive clear, specific instructions and complete their assigned work without interactive dialogue with the user.

## 2. What Tools Do Subagents Have Access To?

Subagents can be configured with flexible tool permissions:

- **Full inheritance**: Omit the `tools` field to grant access to all Claude Code internal tools, including MCP server tools
- **Selective access**: Specify tools as a comma-separated list for granular control
- **Available tools include**: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch, and configured MCP tools
- **Common configurations**:
  - Read-only agents (reviewers, auditors): Read, Grep, Glob
  - Research agents (analysts): Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch
  - Code writers (developers): Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep

## 3. Communication Limitations Between Subagent and Main Agent

**Key limitations:**

- **Unidirectional results**: Subagents work independently and return results to the primary thread—there is no continuous bidirectional communication channel documented
- **No inter-agent coordination**: Subagents do **not** automatically coordinate with each other or share context between their separate spaces
- **No nested delegation**: Subagents **cannot** spawn other subagents, preventing infinite nesting
- **Context compression**: Only relevant information is sent back to the main agent rather than full transcript, to preserve token space

## 4. How Context Works for Subagents

**Context isolation:**

- **Separate context windows**: Each subagent operates with its own isolated context window (200K tokens per subagent)
- **No context pollution**: Task-specific information stays isolated and doesn't pollute the main conversation
- **Resumption support**: Subagents can be resumed with their full transcript file, maintaining previous context across invocations
- **Context preservation**: High-level objectives remain focused in the main thread while detailed work happens in isolated subagent contexts

## Additional Critical Limitations

**Decision-making constraints:**
- Subagents should handle information exploration and answer-specific questions, not primary decision-making or architecture choices
- Without full main agent context, subagent decisions may deviate from intended outcomes unless questions are very clear and specific

**Parallelization constraints:**
- Up to 10 subagents can run concurrently
- When multiple subagents run simultaneously, their answers may contradict each other, creating reliability issues
- Token consumption escalates rapidly: parallel sessions consume 3-4x more tokens than single-threaded interactions

**No nested subagents**: Subagents cannot spawn other subagents

## Recommendation

Given these limitations, subagents work best for:
- Focused, well-defined tasks with clear scope
- Information gathering and analysis (read-only scenarios)
- Specialized tasks that require isolation from main conversation context
- Work that doesn't require continuous user interaction or decision-making

Avoid using subagents for:
- Tasks requiring interactive clarification with users
- Complex decision-making that needs main agent context
- Scenarios requiring inter-agent coordination or communication

Sources:
- [Subagents - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents)
- [Claude Code Frameworks & Sub-Agents: The Complete 2025 Developer's Guide](https://www.medianeth.dev/blog/claude-code-frameworks-subagents-2025)
- [Claude Code: Subagent Deep Dive | Code Centre](https://cuong.io/blog/2025/06/24-claude-code-subagent-deep-dive)
- [Specialized AI Workflows with Claude Code Subagents](https://lgallardo.com/2025/08/02/claude-code-subagents-specialized-workflows/)
